summary: Teaches the preterite 'we' form: -ar verbs use hablamos (same as present), -er verbs change to -imos (comimos), -ir verbs stay the same (vivimos/salimos). Context disambiguates present from past. Also introduces cuando (when), a menudo (often), and siempre (always) in practice sentences.

teachings.summary: For -ar verbs, the preterite 'we' form is identical to the present (hablamos = we speak / we spoke). For -er verbs, present -emos becomes preterite -imos (comemos -> comimos). For -ir verbs, both present and preterite are -imos (vivimos stays vivimos). Context disambiguates.
teachings.rule: -ar: hablamos (present) = hablamos (preterite). -er: comemos (present) -> comimos (preterite). -ir: vivimos (present) = vivimos (preterite). The -er/-ir we-form difference in present (-emos vs -imos) means -er verbs change in preterite but -ir verbs don't.

examples.english: When we speak, I understand everything
When we spoke, I understood everything
We left / We leave (salir: same form)

teachings.notes: The ambiguity of hablamos (present or past) is resolved by context. Words like ayer, mañana, or the tense of surrounding verbs clarify the meaning.

teachings.summary: Recap of all three preterite forms for -ar verbs: hablé (I), habló (he/she), hablamos (we).
Recap of all three preterite forms for -er/-ir verbs: comí (I), comió (he/she), comimos (we).

vocabulary.derivation: menudo is a literary word for small (related to minute/minuscule)
